topic.what: Systematization of 48 peer-reviewed studies on perception-layer attacks against AVs, tracking evolution from single-sensor exploits to multi-sensor fusion (MSF) threats
topic.how: Unified taxonomy of 20 attack vectors organized by sensor type, attack stage, medium, and perception module across 7 dimensions
topic.why_matters: As AVs increasingly fuse camera, LiDAR, and radar for robustness, attackers exploit cross-modal inconsistencies to bypass redundancy checks designed to ensure safety

characteristics.evidence: Final corpus from 124 candidates after three-stage filtering
characteristics.context: 2019-2025 peer-reviewed papers from security, CV, robotics venues

characteristics.evidence: AtkMtd1 to AtkMtd20 covering adversarial patches, 3D objects, signal spoofers
characteristics.context: Across 3 attack mediums and 6 attack classes

characteristics.what_it_measures: Research concentration on single-sensor attacks vs MSF
characteristics.evidence: Table 1 shows 41 studies on single-sensor vs 7 on MSF attacks
characteristics.context: Reveals critical blind spot where most safety-critical component (MSF) receives least scrutiny

characteristics.what_it_measures: Attacks concentrated at input vs processing level
characteristics.evidence: Strong bias toward raw sensor manipulation over algorithmic exploitation
characteristics.context: Insight 2: shift toward deeper algorithmic exploitation is nascent

characteristics.what_it_measures: Studies proposing MSF as unverified defense
characteristics.value: 75% propose no defense or narrow countermeasures; ~33% of defense papers rely on unverified MSF
characteristics.evidence: Only 16/48 papers introduce fusion-aware defenses without empirical validation
characteristics.context: Insight 7: mitigation strategies lag behind evolving attacks

characteristics.evidence: Limited validation under diverse environmental conditions
characteristics.context: Research Gap 3: datasets like KITTI lack geographic/weather diversity

characteristics.what_it_measures: Confidence score of forged phantom object in PoC
characteristics.evidence: Fusion model deceived to accept non-existent phantom pedestrian

novelty: First unified taxonomy spanning single-sensor and MSF attacks across 20 methods
First systematization revealing structural imbalance with MSF being least-examined attack surface
Proof-of-concept demonstrating coordinated IR laser + LiDAR spoofing defeats early fusion
Root cause analysis explaining why research gaps persist (experimental asymmetry, false fusion assumptions, infrastructure limitations)
open_problems: Coordinated cross-modal attacks targeting multiple sensors simultaneously
Longitudinal studies on gradual sensor performance degradation
Real-world validation under diverse weather/lighting conditions
Accessible evaluation infrastructure for MSF attack research
Methods for detecting cross-sensor inconsistencies and fusion logic attacks
